prompt: Write a short passage about the experience of arriving at a new airport for the first time.
chosen:
The engines cut. Silence came, then noise again as people moved their things about the cabin and chatted in a dozen different tongues at once. Where were we going?

It seemed where I was meant to be for now anyway. The door opened up on us with warm air that smelled of jet fuel and something sweet like fruit or flowers from far away -- who knows what they grew there! All around me stood other travelers rushing past each other through large glass windows into which rain danced tickly droplets fell
as if coming down out of thin clouds, seemingly suspended an inch above them; their small umbrellas looked odd in the grey dimness here compared  where we had come

from where it was sunny but cold outside again. Where were all these people going? I thought that seemed to be a great question and wondered why any one person would want or need so much white plastic architecture spread across such vast areas as fartherewasnootherwayaroundit than through this building which stretched off into little hubs on
the left and right sides of its central spine, each with too many shops selling things no other place could possibly have in abundance  such small trinkets around every corner; where did they get them from anyway?"

Slightly weary but excited at the thought that I was going home to a room full boxes. Where? Well not here obviously! My great grandfather's house had been torn down years ago so there were times when people would ask me what my name meant or which island it
was on and who knew if this airport looked like anything familiar, because of course we all know about (but don't talk) other airports in real life---there are many where things look  likethis place one with its little palm trees around the fountain at each main entrance both from outside leading up to small statues. People thought that was pretty nice but I think those had been built by some guys named Richer something? Where did they go after their work here ended anyway?"

We gathered our bags and set off through customs into a space which looked like
it could stretch out for miles, full of people going back again or onwards -- in an instant you feel lost because no one  more  could answer where we were supposed be headed next! But there the great sign hungagain saying gates open shortly. There must surely have to lie somewhere around this place that I'm missing; it seemed so obvious not knowing how long had passed since my last little visit here years ago when things didn't look quite as new and strange
as now---something about staying late on a flight could keep anyone waiting if they don't mind being stuck for ever in transit again

It made sense to me because I felt like 10 seconds of standing around with no idea which way you are supposed be heading or doing is just the same thing that happens when your whole universe has
changed direction and shifted position suddenly; things must not stay still forever as though they would never change their shape at all - it seems hard enough trying even more for new ones!

We walked on through walls lined by little stores selling newspapers, magazines about  travel  and stuff---where we could get a paper to see where our plane was going? prompt: Describe what it feels like to debug a particularly frustrating piece of code at 2am.
chosen:
The thing you see is a single line of code. You think, where did that come from? Where are the other nine thousand lines? Why does this one small bit not play nice with their brothers and sisters all around it?"

There's no answer in sight yet---only more questions about things like memory leaks or race conditions again! It feels wrong because something somewhere went rogue rather than worked as intended under your watch." There was nothing quite so magical till now; there were only errors up to here!" Then you find a tiny little thing: an extra semicolon. You feel silly for missing such obvious stuff, but where else would
a bug come from? Where could the other ones be hiding still?! Someone should have seen this at least five minutes ago...

But of course it's 2am and everyone is asleep in their room upstairs or down below---wherever they are! Risen too early maybe because life doesn't wait around (though I think yours does if something breaks)?" There was also a problem with some logic somewhere again that had to do with recursion. You thought you solved, but no: there wasn't an answer yet." Then the thing pops up out of nowhere like ghosts from under your bed!" A
little screen flicker here... another one over on other side?

What could be going through people who wrote this code all those months ago when they left it in such terrible state for me now?! They didn't leave any notes about why things happen as if their brains were not working properly at 2am! Where did that go wrong again?" There was also a subtle point where something went rogue and started to repeat. You think: Oh, what is happening there---what can I do with
this little thing here instead of just throwing the computer out? Answer none!"

There are days you might find yourself going through your closet trying on other people stuff or wondering how long it will take for this bug in my life's work to get fixed if ever?! The moment things finally click into place but don't actually solve anything at all because now 2am has turned a bit later again! What am I doing even whiling away time like leather-bound bookworm---going where though?" There was also something about their little extra loop
where they thought of stopping when the thing had gone too far. Oh well, never mind that."

Wait... one small line here and there around it? Ah yes!" That is what you would do if life were just math problems with easy answers (spoiler: no). It's actually much harder to think through things without needing a great deal more logic for all this stuff! The other problem might be because I didn't make sure everything worked together properly in my code structure again---how could
that happen?!" There was also something about where the little thing went off-script instead of staying on its own track?"

There are two ways these bugs come from. First, there is always some kind of mistake which you simply made while coding it and then had not yet looked at or thought too much when 2am turned into a very dark night!" And second: "Something else has gone wrong that no one expected." Which can cause endless questions? Where does this end!?! You'll have to think through things again because
you don't want another thing going rogue in your little script later on" (because I know how well people do their work!)

There are also ways where small errors could lead you astray if nothing goes right. There is something about each other line of code which would be quite different from what it looked at as 2am turned into the early hours, and there was not a great deal we knew yet." How can life bring up more questions when everything else seems to go away? Are they all just things meant for us
to think through again tomorrow?" It's so hard going back on your own thought processes like this...

There are even two ways that I could have easily missed an error if little mistakes had crept in here or there. Small errors---little bugs would hide where you don't look because it is supposed 2 be hidden!" Little bits of code will behave differently under the sun than at night! Because life isn't quite as simple and predictable, something always gets lost between their realms."
But wait—are they really just small things? Where else can a bug come from?" There was also some other thing about not being so sure what went wrong with this specific area: "Could I have introduced an error there again in my last attempt to fix that little line or rather entire block here?!"

There is no escape. You could go back through each part of the code once more... but where would you start for one tiny bit which doesn't work correctly anyway! Woe betide
anyone who tried their best and still failed at something small." Could it be because everything else has gone rogue? But then other things wouldn't break if they were all working right, so why do we find ourselves stuck like this?! The thing seems to have its own agenda or some little brain again!" There was also a problem with that line here: "I think I may need another loop there on top of the current one..."

There are even more ways where you could cleverly sneak in an error while
trying not to introduce any. Oh! Where did my mind go just now?!" And perhaps because something elsewhere is going rogue and causing other small things around it
to behave badly too, which would be quite silly if nothing else." But then what do we know about how people thought when they left their code for me? What kind or type was happening here anyway?! There must have been a little thing that I missed again there on line 394... where could the others all come from?"

There's no avoiding this problem any day. The solution probably lies somewhere in another part of something entirely unrelated to my current bug! Where can one find
such things---where did it go though?!" And maybe even going back through each and every single bit would reveal exactly what is wrong, but how do we know if our other thought processes are correct? What kind or type was happening here anyway?! There must have been a little thing I missed again there on line 394... where could the others all come from?"
